codeReview.summary: Implementation is complete and correct. All 5 expectedBehavior items are implemented: Per-concept accuracy breakdown, Misconception detection, Remediation queue generation, Add to review queue action, and Export/share results. Code is well-structured with proper TypeScript types, uses useMemo/useCallback for performance, and follows project conventions.

issues.description: addToReviewQueue function doesn't check if the concept is already in the review queue before adding. While addAllWeakToQueue properly checks with .first(), this single-item function doesn't. This could lead to duplicate entries if the user clicks 'Add to queue' multiple times rapidly.

MISCONCEPTION_DESCRIPTIONS only covers 11 specific misconception tags. If a question has a misconceptionTag that isn't in this map, it will still be displayed but without a human-readable description or indicators - just the raw tag name.

The Study link in remediation items always navigates to /course-map unless item.type === 'lecture'. For 'example', 'worksheet', 'instruction' types, it doesn't construct proper deep links. This may be intentional as those routes may not be implemented yet.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature was assigned 'backend-worker' skill but the work was actually implementing a React UI component (ResultsSummary.tsx). The backend-worker skill focuses on data processing scripts, not React components. This mismatch didn't cause issues as the worker followed proper React/TypeScript conventions, but suggests the skill assignment may have been incorrect.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Feature description: 'Build ResultsSummary component' - this is a UI component implementation, not data processing. The backend-worker SKILL.md procedure focuses on 'scripts/', 'data/processed/', and 'validation logic'.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker correctly followed established patterns for React components (useMemo for computed values, useCallback for handlers, proper TypeScript typing) and integrated with existing infrastructure (Dexie DB, existing DrillMode patterns). No convention violations observed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: ResultsSummary.tsx: Uses cn() utility, lucide-react icons, proper component structure, follows the same patterns as DrillMode.tsx

sharedStateObservations.observation: The MISCONCEPTION_DESCRIPTIONS map was defined with hardcoded values. The worker didn't iterate through actual misconceptionTags in question-bank.json to discover all possible values. A more robust approach would be to generate the descriptions map from the actual data.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: MISCONCEPTION_DESCRIPTIONS has 11 entries but question-bank.json may have different or additional misconceptionTags

summary: PASS - Implementation is complete with all 5 expectedBehavior items delivered. Code quality is good with proper TypeScript types and React patterns. Non-blocking issues include missing duplicate-check in addToReviewQueue and hardcoded misconception descriptions that may not cover all cases. The skill mismatch (backend-worker vs UI component) didn't impact output quality.
